chief_complaint: A 6-year-old boy presents with seizures and a port-wine stain on his face.
history: The patient has a history of seizures, glaucoma, limb hypertrophy, and venous anomalies. He has a facial port-wine stain and truncal port-wine stains. Exome sequencing did not reveal any pathogenic variants.
exam: Vital signs: normal. Physical examination reveals a facial port-wine stain, truncal port-wine stains, limb hypertrophy, and venous anomalies.
labs: Imaging studies confirmed the coexistence of Sturge-Weber syndrome and Klippel-Trenaunay syndrome phenotypes. No pathogenic variants were found on exome sequencing.
